overview.problem: The existing budget app has a basic, unpolished UI that doesn't provide an at-a-glance financial overview or modern design language.
overview.solution: Redesign the app dashboard to match the Fortuna reference design — a polished wealth overview with bento grid layout, Material Design 3 colors, spending categories, budget tracking, and quick insights.
overview.target_user: Individuals who want a clean, modern budgeting app with instant visibility into their financial health.
overview.success_metric: The dashboard renders all key financial metrics (net worth, budget progress, top categories, insights) in a visually polished bento grid layout matching the reference HTML.

screens.name: Dashboard (Home)
screens.purpose: Main screen showing total net worth, monthly budget progress, top spending categories, quick insights, and security health in a bento grid layout.

screens.name: Transaction History
screens.purpose: View and manage all income/expense transactions with delete capability.

features.name: Dashboard Overview
features.description: Bento grid dashboard showing total net worth with trend indicator, monthly budget progress bar, top spending categories with percentage bars, quick insights (savings goal, bills due), and security health score.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user opens the app, when the dashboard loads, then the total net worth card displays with a trend indicator showing percentage change from last month.
Given monthly budget data exists, when the dashboard renders, then the budget card shows spent amount vs total budget with a progress bar and remaining amount.
Given transactions exist, when the dashboard renders, then top spending categories display with category icons, names, percentages, and progress bars.
Given the dashboard loads, when rendered, then quick insights section shows savings goal amount and bills due countdown.
Given the dashboard loads, when rendered, then security health section shows a circular score indicator with status text.

features.name: Transaction Management
features.description: Add, view, and delete income/expense transactions. Persists via SharedPreferences. Existing BudgetModel logic is preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Given the user fills the transaction form, when they submit, then the transaction is saved to SharedPreferences and appears in the list.
Given transactions exist, when the user views the history, then all transactions are displayed with amount, description, and income/expense indicator.
Given a transaction exists, when the user deletes it, then it is removed from SharedPreferences and the list updates.
Given the user enters an amount, when submitting, then positive amounts are income and negative amounts are expenses.

features.name: Bottom Navigation
features.description: Mobile bottom nav bar with Home, History, Add, and Budgets tabs. Desktop FAB for adding transactions.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app runs on mobile width, when rendered, then a bottom nav bar is visible with Home, History, Add, and Budgets icons.
Given the user taps a nav item, when selected, then the active tab is highlighted and navigates to the corresponding screen.
Given the app runs on desktop width, when rendered, then a floating action button is visible in the bottom-right corner.

features.name: Fortuna Design System
features.description: Apply the Fortuna Material Design 3 color palette, typography scale, and component styling from the reference HTML throughout the app.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app renders, when any screen is displayed, then all colors match the Fortuna MD3 palette (primary #041627, surface #f7f9fb, error #ba1a1a, etc.).
Given the app renders, when text is displayed, then typography uses the Inter font family with the defined size/weight scale.
Given cards render, when displayed, then they use rounded corners (xl/2xl), subtle shadows, and surface-container-lowest backgrounds.

features.name: Animations & Micro-interactions
features.description: Entrance animations for dashboard cards and scroll shadow effect on the top app bar.
acceptance_criteria: Given the dashboard loads, when the screen renders, then cards animate in with staggered fade-up transitions.
Given the user scrolls down, when scroll position > 10px, then the top app bar gains a shadow.

data.backend_notes: Uses SharedPreferences for local transaction storage. No cloud sync.

out_of_scope: Dark mode toggle (colors are light-mode only from reference)
Budget detail page (button can be placeholder)
